lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: I've been better...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to your foot?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: I was minding my own business in that abandoned shack for the night and suddenly I could hear...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A g-g-g-ghost?!

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: No... Don't interrupt!

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Where was I?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: That was it... a faint sound... wind chimes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please don't tell me you were attacked by the wind.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: What did I say about interruptions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: The chiming grew louder and started to sound like the knocking of bones. I was in the bed hiding under the moth-eaten sheet - ignorance is bliss, y'know? - and suddenly the sheet was pulled off of me and there was

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: a-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A skeleton!

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: I SAID NO INTER- Oh yes, that's correct... Skeletons. Two of the blighters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you seem to have got away in one piece!

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: One piece?! Do you not-

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: THE BONEY BLIGHTER BIT MY FOOT!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry... You didn't leave anything behind did you? I'm a brave adventurer! I'm sure I can pry whatever it is from their malicious mitts.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: A few pots, but without my tools they won'y do me any good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Me? Just a tinker with nothing to trade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to your wares?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: A tinker doesn't get the respect that they used to... Gah! A thief took my bag in the night, they took everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry to hear you've had some bad luck of late, did you lose much?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Bits and pieces, nothing too important I s'pose... An old pan flute, a dirty book and a charred skull.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: That's all in the past now, anyway... I'm afraid I can't help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's alright, I need no help.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Didn't someone send you for the pots?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope, just exploring...

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Well, if anyone asks... I'm dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye.

sections.title: Kharedst's memoirs (with Jewellery of jubilation included)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you the tinker this entry mentions?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Oh, me? Oh my... Goodness gracious what...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A life?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: What? No...

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Well, I s'pose that was me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's so surprising?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: How did you find me!? Was it you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was it me what? I just found this journal... I didn't write it.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: I remember this day... How could I forget.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Are you saying the man I sold those trinkets to was the king?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... You didn't know?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Those were dark objects, corrupted with powerful magic. Dark magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's all in the past now. I don't think there's any reason to worry about it.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: No, no, no... this ain't good. They'll find me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who will find you?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: You already know too much. But you don't know my name. Good. Let's keep it that way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if I find out anything useful, I'll come let you know, alright?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: If you can find me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Deal...

sections.title: Any item worth one million coins or more on the Grand Exchange

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, what?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: THANKS!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I didn't mean for you to keep it.

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Then why are you handing it to me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone made me do it!

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: It's alright mate! We've all been there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Existential Crisis?

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: It'll pass! Anyhow, I'll be seeing you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure...

lines.speaker: Old dwarf
lines.text: I'm afraid I've nothing to trade for that.
